newsSinceJun6: No material new catalysts since June 6. Today's -3.9% (to $9.31) is newsless drift, a continuation of pressure from Jefferies' mid-May Underperform initiation ($8 PT, 'structural challenges' / customer-concentration warnings), not fresh fundamentals. Soft-positive items only: Frost & Sullivan 2026 Global ADAS Company of the Year award and an upcoming June 18 AGM. The robotics thesis pillars are unchanged: $900M Mentee Robotics acquisition (closed Q1 2026), first on-site humanoid proof-of-concept deployments still guided for 2026, Q1 revenue +27% YoY with raised FY26 guidance midpoint, and a $250M buyback authorization.

sources.label: Mobileye news releases / Frost & Sullivan ADAS award context (Simply Wall St)
Jefferies Underperform, $8 PT, 24% downside warning (Yahoo Finance)
Mobileye to Acquire Mentee Robotics (Mobileye blog)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already promoted MBLY to #9 on exactly this setup (under-ownership, -double-digit YTD, direct humanoid stack via Mentee); nothing genuinely new has landed since June 6, so re-moving would be churn. Today's -3.9% newsless slide with the thesis intact marginally improves asymmetry, worth a small positive drift, not another rank move.
verdicts.bull: An $8B-cap company with a closed $900M humanoid platform (Mentee), Mobileye-scale AI training infra, +27% YoY revenue, raised FY26 guidance and a $250M buyback is priced near sell-side worst case; first humanoid PoC deployments in 2026 are a free option the market is assigning roughly zero.
verdicts.neutral: The tape is fighting the thesis: Jefferies' structural-challenge bear case ($8 PT) is anchoring the stock and there is no dated humanoid catalyst between now and the June 18 AGM, so the position is dead money until a Mentee deployment or design-win headline.
verdicts.bear: Mentee proves a sub-scale science project versus Tesla/Figure/Unitree, the core ADAS franchise keeps losing content to in-house OEM and Chinese stacks per the Jefferies thesis, and the stock grinds toward $8 with the humanoid option expiring worthless.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: First Mentee humanoid on-site proof-of-concept customer deployment (guided for 2026), or a named pilot customer/order announcement.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Jefferies-style structural erosion of the core ADAS business (customer concentration, OEM in-housing) overwhelming the humanoid optionality before it can be demonstrated.
